architecture.title: How revocation stages work differently
architecture.subtitle: Switch credential state to see how each architecture responds

differences.text: Traditional: biometrics are processed first; credential is checked last. A revoked user still triggers the full biometric pipeline.

PRISM: the blockchain gate fires first. If the DID is revoked, the smart contract reverts the transaction. Since PRISM uses 1:1 matching, the profile to match can never be found and the biometric gate never initializes.

hypothesis.title: Hypothesis framework
hypothesis.subtitle: The test measures how much acceptance drops when a credential is revoked

null.label: H₀ — Null
null.text: There is no significant difference between the proposed hierarchical biometric–blockchain architecture and the parallel fusion architecture in the degree to which credential revocation reduces the probability of attendance verification for enrolled users.

alternative.label: H₁ — Alternative
alternative.text: There is a significant difference between the proposed hierarchical biometric–blockchain architecture and the parallel fusion architecture in the degree to which credential revocation reduces the probability of attendance verification for enrolled users (p < 0.05).

measurements.text: How much the acceptance rate drops when a credential is revoked
Bigger drop → stronger revocation enforcement → better security
No drop → revocation has no effect → security failure

hypothesis.why_prism: Because PRISM checks the DID on-chain before biometrics run, a revoked credential causes a transaction revert at step 2. Hence, the acceptance probability collapses to P(verify | revoked) = 0. The parallel baseline has no such pre-gate, so revoked users can still pass biometric matching.

factorial.title: 2×2 factorial design
factorial.subtitle: Architecture × Credential State
factorial.reference_note: Reference group: Parallel architecture in the active state (n₁₁). All comparisons are made relative to this cell.
factorial.stat_reason: Used because PRISM + Revoked (n₂₂) is expected to produce complete separation, as in zero verifications. Standard logistic regression fails here; Firth's penalty stabilizes the estimate.

stat_cards.name: Standard error
rows: Firth's penalty shrinks SE at boundaries
